The Destination

Why Mazatlán

A City the World is Rediscovering

Mazatlán is not a new discovery — it's a rediscovery. Long before Los Cabos and decades before Puerto Vallarta became a household name, Mazatlán was already Mexico's crown jewel on the Pacific. In its colonial era, it was the country's most important Pacific port, attracting European merchants, traders, and adventurers who left behind an architectural legacy unique to Mexico's coastline. Today it holds the largest intact colonial historic district of any oceanfront city in all of Latin America — cobblestone streets, painted facades, and world-class restaurants that rival the most storied quarters of Europe.

Hollywood's Favorite Secret

In the 1940s and 50s, Mazatlán was the escape of choice for America's elite. John Wayne, Gary Cooper and the Bing Crosby crowd made the journey south, drawn by the sport fishing and a city that knew how to live well. Mazatlán was the original Mexican Riviera — where glamour met the sea long before the term "resort destination" existed. That spirit never left, it simply waited for the world to catch up.

Where Culture Meets the Pacific

Mazatlán's Carnival is the third largest in the world — behind only Rio de Janeiro and New Orleans — celebrated continuously since 1898. Over a million people line the Malecón each year. The city is also the acknowledged Sailfish Capital of the World, and its food scene has been declared among the most underrated in Mexico by Food & Wine, Condé Nast Traveler, and the New York Times. This is not a city on the rise. It is a city arriving.

The Numbers Tell the Story

Puerto Vallarta now commands $800–$1,200 per square foot for comparable luxury oceanfront product. Mazatlán is not there yet — and that is precisely the opportunity. This is where Puerto Vallarta was fifteen years ago, before the market recognized what was there. Direct flights from Los Angeles, Phoenix, Seattle, Denver, and Houston have expanded dramatically.

The Mexican federal government has invested over $500 million USD in infrastructure — the Malecón expansion, a new convention center, and the full revitalization of the historic center. Luxury real estate in the Malecón corridor has posted double-digit annual appreciation for five consecutive years. Early movers in Puerto Vallarta and Los Cabos made generational returns. Mazatlán is that market, right now.

The Development

Where Elegance
Meets the Pacific

Stelarhe is the most innovative and exclusive residential complex in Mazatlán and northwestern Mexico — two luxury towers of 30 levels each, rising directly on the Malecón, the longest seafront promenade in Latin America. World-class amenities, over 20 floor plans, and an incomparable Pacific horizon.

Elite Fitness Center
Rooftop Terrace & Bar
Luxury Lobby

The Property

Unit C1 — 27th Floor

A rare 2-bedroom, 2.5-bath residence on the 27th floor, commanding panoramic Pacific and Malecón views. One of only 16 units in the entire complex — 10 penthouses and 6 select residences — granted exclusive access to the premium 2nd-bridge amenity deck.

★ Exclusive — 2nd Bridge Access

Unit C1 is among only 16 units in all of Stelarhe granted private access to the 2nd bridge amenity deck — a premium pool, fitness facilities, and social spaces reserved exclusively for penthouses and select residences. This distinction is not available to the general building population and adds significant rental and resale premium.

📍 Pre-Construction Advantage

The contract price is fixed at developer pre-construction pricing. At December 2026 completion, market value is expected to exceed the current contract price — creating built-in appreciation before the first offer is received. The Fideicomiso (Mexican bank trust) provides the legal framework protecting all parties.

🔑 Investment Status

A substantial deposit is already in place. The investor bridges the remaining gap to the 80% threshold required to list the property for sale. The unit can be listed within 30–60 days of funding — no construction wait required.

The Investment

Two Paths.
One Exceptional Asset.

A deposit is in place. The investor provides ~$215,000 USD, secured by Fideicomiso and held in Mexican escrow against the property. Upon funding, the unit lists immediately. The property closes in December 2026. The investor receives full principal plus profit share when the unit is ultimately sold.
